Mas afinal,quem é Petra Costa? De onde surgiu? Como consegue expressar sua ideologia e ganhar espaços com tanta visibilidade? 
Pois bem.. Sempre que alguém tem uma notoriedade não construída com o suor de seu trabalho,akgo está errado.   Quando se investiga e se encontra a fonte da riqueza financeira justificando uma escalada meteórica,temos que pensar a quem e a quais interesses determinada pessoa está servindo. E aqui se torna ben claro quando essa moça surgiu assim,com num passe de mágica durante o início do governo Bolsonaro. Sabemos da inconformidade da esquerda pela verdadeira alternância de poder revelada e evidente nas democracias verdeiras.Essas não costumam atacar um adversário vitorioso. Não foi o caso do Presidente Bolsonaro que venceu apesar de tudo incluindo uma facada que quase lhe tirou a visa. Perra Costa é uma consequência dessa inconformidade da esquerda. 
Quem é Petra Costa? Uma cineasta à serviço da militância de esquerda. Possui ligações com ONGs e instituições internacionais para promover as agendas identitarias verdadeiras ideologias radicais incluindo Open Society Foundations (George Soros.Também o Sundance Institute
Participou de programas do Sundance Documentary Film Program, que oferece,claro, bolsas e mentorias para documentaristas com projetos daquilo que eles selecionam como de relevância social dentro da doutrinação de esquerda 
Faz "propaganda política" e tem vínculos com a esquerda Defende o Wokismo. Fomento via  fundos globais para diversidade e justiça social que o wokismo distorce capturando para si o conceito de direitos humanos e democracia quando na verdade promovem conflitos e disputas entre cor, raça e sexo e não se aborrecem em proteger os verdadeiros governantes autoritários.
Alguns críticos apontam que há afinidades ideológicas entre os temas abordados por Petra e as causas defendidas pela Open Society. Embora não haja prova direta de financiamento da Open Society para Democracia em Vertigem, o tipo de produção que ela realiza (documentários com foco em direitos humanos e democracia dentro daquilo que os wokes querem impor e não com a verdadeira democracia e direitos humanos) é alinhado com os objetivos da fundação. comment_text:
I invite you to view the documentary "Tropical Apocalypse," a compelling work by Pietra Costa that explores the significant growth of evangelical Christianity in Brazil and its increasing influence on the country's political landscape. The film provides valuable historical context, offering insights into the complex interplay between religious beliefs and political power. In particular, it sheds light on the similarities observed in the relationships between Brazilian and U.S. presidents during the period under examination.

comment_text: Dear Netflix Team, I'm writing to you today as a dedicated Netflix subscriber and an avid fan of animation. While I greatly appreciate the diverse range of content you offer, I believe there's a significant, underserved market segment that Netflix is uniquely positioned to capture, which could lead to substantial growth and deeper customer loyalty. My observation, shared by many in online communities, is a growing demand for unique, community-driven animated series – particularly those with artistic styles and narrative depths similar to breakout independent hits like The Amazing Digital Circus and Hazbin Hotel. These shows, often incubated on platforms like YouTube, demonstrate an incredible ability to cultivate passionate, highly engaged fanbases. Currently, these fans often experience "subscription fatigue" by needing to navigate various platforms or fragmented channels to find and support the content they truly love. This creates an opportunity for Netflix. Consider the following potential benefits for Netflix and its subscribers: •  Tapping into a Passionate Niche: These communities are not just passive viewers; they are active participants, eager to invest in and champion the content they adore. By recognizing and catering to this demand, Netflix could attract and retain a highly loyal, dedicated subscriber base.  •  Cost-Effective Content Incubation: Instead of solely investing in high-budget, broad-appeal productions, Netflix could partner with these independent creators and their established communities. Imagine a model where Netflix provides exposure and a streamlined platform for these shows, while the community actively contributes to funding and promoting them (e.g., through direct subscriptions to creator channels or other innovative support mechanisms).  •  Diversifying the Content Portfolio: Integrating such unique, often experimental, animation styles would enrich Netflix's library, offering content that stands out from competitors and appeals to viewers seeking something truly different.  •  Becoming an Industry Leader in Fan-Driven Content: By embracing this model, Netflix could pioneer a new way of content acquisition and distribution, becoming the go-to platform for animation that truly resonates with its core audience.   My suggestion is to explore a more decentralized, "à la carte" or "channel subscription" approach for these specific animation categories. This would allow individual creators or small studios to operate dedicated "channels" within Netflix's ecosystem. Netflix could then leverage its immense promotional power to highlight these channels, driving traffic and direct financial support from passionate communities to the creators. In return, Netflix could benefit from increased user engagement, new subscriber acquisition, and potentially a share of the revenue generated through these unique community-supported models. This approach could alleviate subscriber frustration by centralizing access to these desired shows, while simultaneously empowering creators and proving Netflix's commitment to truly innovative and diverse storytelling driven by genuine market demand. Thank you for considering this perspective. I believe this represents a significant opportunity for Netflix to innovate and further solidify its position as a global leader in entertainment. Sincerely,
